calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-11-2018 Set For Case Management Conference On Jun-27-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2017 Continued To May-14-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, James C. Ashworth of the law offices of James C. Ashworth (530-574-1130) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jason J. Granskog of the law offices of Bowles & Verna, LLP (925-935-3300) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS NEVILLE PARUJANWALA and NICK MCTOJO, INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all five causes of action in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Avignon Julian, LLC is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for professional negligence and overruled as to all other causes of action. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that defendants misrepresented that there was a settlement and plaintiff justifiably relied on those misrepresentations. The fact that there was no written settlement agreement or compliance with CCP 664.6 does not preclude a cognizable claim. Parties can settle a case without a written agreement or complying with CCP 664.6. Even if the court takes judicial notice of the Tse declaration, that declaration fails to show that the misrepresentation claims fail. Ms. Tse's understanding is not an admission by plaintiff. Reliance is a factual issue not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Plaintiff adequately alleges facts that show a duty to disclose based on partial disclosure which supports its constructive fraud cause of action. A duty to disclose may arise where the defendant has exclusive knowledge of facts that are not accessible to the plaintiff, where the defendant has actively concealed information from the plaintiff, or where the defendant partially suppresses the facts. (5 Witkin, Summary of CA Law (10th edition 2016) "Torts" 793-799.). The professional negligence cause of action fails because defendants (buyer's agents) did not owe a professional duty to plaintiff. In addition, the professional negligence claim merely repeats the fraud claims. Plaintiff's fifth cause of action, while incorrectly titled common count, alleges a cognizable claim for restitution/quasi contract. (McBride v. Boughton (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 379, 388.) The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No: 8098, email: casey.giselle@gmail.com. (D302)
